concept.core_loop: One-thumb: tap to hop the hero one lane forward; swipe left/right to sidestep and line up a gap. Cross several lanes of hazards (each lane scrolls at its own speed/direction) to reach the top edge for a point, then the field resets one lane deeper and the run continues. The live decision every second is reading the lanes and timing the hop — dash through a closing gap for a speed-bonus, or sidestep and wait for a safe one. One hazard contact ends the run (tap to restart). Difficulty ramps every ~3 crossings: lanes move faster and hazards pack tighter, up to a cap.
mechanics: multi-lane hazard spawning with per-lane speed + direction
speed-cross streak bonus (reach the top quickly / without backtracking -> bonus points)
difficulty ramp (lane speed + hazard density tiers, capped)
concept.art_direction: PIXEL-ART / retro representational — this is the M1.5 raster STYLE-B target (NOT geometric/SVG, and deliberately the HARD-EDGED opposite of creature-0001's soft painterly look): a chunky pixel hero character, blocky pixel hazard creatures/carts, a tiled pixel landscape of grass/path lanes. Builder placeholder (primitives, raster re-skin later with a pixel-art profile + nearest-filter import): a limited bright retro palette (NES-ish grass-green / dirt-brown lanes, hero in one bright accent like cyan or red), hard-edged rectangles snapped to a coarse grid (evoking big pixels), flat alternating lane bands, chunky snap-hop motion (no smooth easing — discrete steps), feedback: a hard 1-frame white flash + short sharp shake on hit, a crisp pop + score tick on each crossing.
concept.differentiation_notes: Distinct from the runner (side auto-scroll), shooter (top-down combat), match-3, and creature-0001 (free-roam soft dodge): a discrete-hop, lane-timing crossing game with no weapons. Its hook is being the set's PIXEL-ART/retro representational title (M1.5 style B), a hard-edged look chosen to contrast maximally with creature-0001's painterly creatures — the two together are the 'one system, two visibly distinct styles' evidence the milestone needs.

theme.premise: a tiny hopper dashing across an endless stream of hazard lanes in a snappy retro arcade world
theme.setting: a rural roadside of grass fields and dirt lanes under flat midday light

issues: asset: within-game visual INCOHESION (styled A/B, 2026-06-01) — generated sprites read as different worlds: a sci-fi CYAN ROBOT hero vs fantasy RED OGRE/demon hazards. Each sprite subject is derived independently with no shared character/world design language; concept.theme (bright punchy 8-bit retro arcade roadside) does not pin a unifying character bible. FIX: asset SKILL.md must derive a shared world/character design language from concept.theme and constrain EVERY sprite recipe to it (one world, one character family).

asset: background not re-skinned — asset_pass.recipes=[hero,hazard] only; BG is still primitive flat lane bands (no background art asset), so sprites float on untextured primitives, breaking the one-rendered-system goal. NOTE: backgrounds are M1.7-deferred scope, but their absence undercuts cohesion now — pull a background recipe into asset scope or accelerate M1.7.

audio: SFX character wrong for theme (audio A/B 2026-06-01) — even in the bright 8-bit arcade theme the SFX are "strange, aggressive sudden explosions" (owner). concept.theme asks for crisp chiptune (pop + score tick, 8-bit), not explosions. CONTRAST RESULT vs creature: the SAME explosive palette is wrong for BOTH a cozy-woodland and an arcade theme — confirming the audio skill defaults to a generic aggressive/explosive SFX character instead of DERIVING from concept.theme. FIX: audio SKILL.md must derive SFX sonic character from concept.theme.

audio: BGM CONFIRMED NOT PLAYING (probe 2026-06-01: MusicAmbient.playing=false, pos=0 after 90 frames; corroborated — owner heard SFX but not the equal-level -14 dBFS bgm). ROOT CAUSE: the looping bed play() is called synchronously inside _ready right after add_child (_setup_audio Main.gd:166-177), which does not reliably start an AudioStreamPlayer that frame; SFX work because they fire later in gameplay. FIX: audio SKILL.md should start the bed via autoplay=true (set before add_child) or a deferred/awaited play(), not an immediate in-_ready play().

visual_system.world_bible: A bright, punchy 8-bit retro-arcade roadside world — a sunny field of grass and dirt lanes under flat midday light, the look of one single retro arcade cartridge. ONE character family: chunky, blocky, big-eyed cartoon CREATURES (blob-monsters), all rendered in the same limited bright NES palette with hard pixel edges, thick readable silhouettes and flat cel shading. NO mixing of registers (the A/B-round-1 cyan-ROBOT-vs-red-DEMON incohesion: one mechanical/sci-fi, one organic/fantasy = two different worlds). The hero is a small, friendly, round CYAN blob-creature (a plucky little hopper); the hazards are bigger, grumpy RED blob-creatures of the EXACT same cartoon family (same eyes, same outline, same shading) — bigger and angrier so danger reads at a glance. Cohesion via shared creature family + pixel style + palette + flat light; contrast via color role (cyan hero vs red hazard) + size + expression. Both are creatures of one cartoon arcade world — never one mechanical and one organic.
visual_system.form: hard-edged blocky pixel-art sprites; one cartoon blob-creature family — a small round cyan blob hero vs. a big stocky red blob hazard, same eye/outline/shading language — deliberate size + complementary cyan/red color contrast so threat reads instantly
visual_system.shading: flat limited-palette pixel shading, crisp hard pixel edges (no gradients/antialias); procedural flat lane bands + faint pixel grid behind
visual_system.scale: 1024 square masters downscaled to 128px on import (process/size_limit=128), drawn small with NEAREST filter (no mipmaps) = crisp pixels; hero ~64px centered on its cell; each variable-width hazard bar is filled with N=round(w/64) exact-fit demon units so visual width == collision width and no sprite is ever stretched
visual_system.prompt_scaffold: pixel art sprite, pixelated, 8-bit retro video game character, crisp hard-edged chunky pixels, limited bright retro NES palette, clean single centered subject, full body, flat colors, clean transparent background,

style.style_prompt: pixel art sprite, pixelated, 8-bit retro video game character, crisp hard-edged chunky pixels, limited bright retro NES palette

asset_pass.notes: A/B-ROUND-3 ART RE-PASS (2026-06-02, GPU-validated). Addresses the round-2 owner verdict ('art terrible/not great'). Reframe (round-3 probe): sprites were already fine; the problems were a flat near-black background void + a hero too small vs the red hazards. Fixes: (a) NEW themed BACKGROUND (M1.7-deferred capability, now in scope) — a full-frame pixel-art sunny-roadside via the plain sdxl template (layerdiffuse:false, 768x1344, seed 720); drawn as the bottom layer in _draw_background (guarded), framing the centered Frogger playfield instead of black void. NOTE: the opaque sdxl template has no LoRA node, so the pixel-art-xl LoRA can't apply to the background; the pixel look comes from prompt terms + the project's NEAREST texture_filter (set on the Main node, inherited at draw time) — validated good in-frame. (b) HERO SIZING: HERO_DRAW 64->80 px (visual only; collision HERO_SIZE=42 untouched) so the hero reads larger than a single 64px hazard unit. NEW FINDING (recorded for the asset skill): for a full-PLAYFIELD game like a lane-crosser, a background image only enriches the MARGINS — the core playfield (flat lane bands) still reads flat against the rich backdrop. Fully fixing that needs lane-TILE textures (a different technique: draw_texture_rect tiled per lane), which also risks lane-readability and so is deferred to an owner-directed follow-up rather than done speculatively. Sprites (hero, hazard) UNCHANGED from round-2. selftest SELFTEST OK; headless import clean; logic untouched. Owner aesthetic A/B pending (Phase-2 playtest). --- PRIOR (A/B-ROUND-2 RE-SKIN, 2026-06-01) — fixes the round-1 cyan-ROBOT-vs-red-DEMON incohesion (sci-fi vs fantasy = two worlds) by deriving a world_bible FIRST (see visual_system.world_bible: ONE cartoon blob-creature family) and regenerating BOTH actors as members of it. hero REGENERATED: round-1 was a mechanical cyan robot; round-2 hero is a cute round CYAN blob-creature (seed 410, accepted pass 1). hazard REGENERATED as the hero's red sibling — same blocky cartoon blob family, big/angry/red so danger reads at a glance. Hazard iteration (6 passes — the single-subject + color constraints fought each other): red-blob+stray-2nd-creature+shadow -> red-blob with white-alpha-hole -> clean GREEN blob (wrong color role) -> red TOMATO (literal, my 'tomato-red' wording) -> GREY blob (over-negation) -> [KEPT seed 420] clean single bright-RED blob, stocky/snarling, no shadow/hole/extra. Lesson folded: name the COLOR plainly ('bright red', not 'tomato-red') and don't over-stack color negatives. Style B (hard-edged pixel-art retro) via ComfyUI + SDXL Juggernaut v9 + pixel-art-xl LoRA + LayerDiffuse (RGBA). Still deliberate maximal contrast to creature-0001's soft painterly woodland = the 'one system, two visibly distinct identities' milestone evidence. hero + hazard both raster, drawn via draw_texture_rect in the existing immediate-mode _draw() (z-order/shake/hop-pop preserved). KEY TECHNIQUE — variable-width hazard: the game scrolls hazard bars of continuous width (CELL..CELL*3); a fixed-aspect sprite would stretch and break the pixel grid, so each bar is filled with N=round(w/HAZ_UNIT) exact-fit demon units (uw=w/N) — a 'pack of monsters' that keeps square pixels and makes visual width == collision width. PIXEL CRISPNESS: 1024 masters are downscaled to 128px on import (size_limit) then drawn at 64px (clean ÷2) with TEXTURE_FILTER_NEAREST + mipmaps off. Lanes/goal/HUD/pixel-grid stay procedural (flat retro bands — correct for the aesthetic, recorded as left_primitive). Both sprites accepted on pass 1 (the LoRA nailed the look). Folded creature-0001 findings: constrained single-centered-subject prompts + anti-smooth negatives; mixed-method awareness (here both actors suit raster, the flat lanes suit primitive). Visual-verified via SceneTree screenshot harness (real Vulkan renderer, RTX 5080). Selftest still SELFTEST OK; headless run clean; logic untouched. STATUS HELD AT validated (validated->styled illegal): awaits owner ~60s playtest -> playable, then human A/B + IP-safety -> styled. IP-safety: generic descriptors only, no franchise/character/studio names; every recipe negative excludes logo/watermark/text/trademarked-character/brand/celebrity.

audio_system.sonic_character: crisp clean 8-bit chiptune timbre — square/triangle-wave blips, bleeps and chimes with tight clean chip transients, NES-ish; the chip attack is clean and bright, NOT an explosive/noise-burst/realistic transient. SFX draw their material from this vocabulary (the audio sibling of the visual world_bible) — the round-1 'strange aggressive explosions' came from the SFX defaulting to generic explosive transients instead of this chip character.

audio_pass.notes: A/B-ROUND-3 AUDIO RE-PASS (2026-06-02, GPU-validated calibration). Addresses the round-2 owner verdict (SFX 'explosive', BGM 'weak'). Root cause (shared with creature; docs/superpowers/2026-06-02-audio-art-probe-results.md): explosive SFX = steps≈8 broadband noise, NOT prompt. Regenerated all 4 SFX as clean square-wave chiptune at steps 100 (objective check: ZCR 1400-3900/s clean; gameover trimmed to 0.54s by the envelope; streak is a deliberately-bright ascending arpeggio so its ZCR runs higher). The deterministic loudness/trim envelope is now AUTO-APPLIED by comfy.mjs genAudio for kind:sfx (SFX hit ~RMS 0.13). BGM: regenerated as a mellow chiptune MELODY (cfg 6, anti-drone negatives, winning probe seed 202) — chiptune is naturally melodic so it only needed de-harshening. LEVELS: the chiptune bed is dense/loud (~RMS 0.20, ~4 dB ABOVE the SFX), so volume_db CUT to -12 (round-2 was -4) to sit ~8-9 dB under. Wiring (bed rebuild + autoplay) unchanged from round-2. Owner ear-judgment pending (Phase-2 playtest). --- PRIOR (A/B-ROUND-2 AUDIO RE-PASS, 2026-06-01). (1) SFX TIMBRE FIX: derived audio_system.sonic_character from concept.theme (crisp clean 8-bit chiptune — square/triangle blips, clean chip transients) and regenerated all 4 SFX with it threaded into every prompt PLUS theme-aware negatives ('explosion, noise burst, harsh, distortion, aggressive, realistic') that round 1 lacked — addresses the round-1 'strange aggressive sudden explosions' finding (the SFX defaulted to generic explosive transients in BOTH this arcade theme and the cozy creature theme = the skill wasn't theme-bound). (2) BGM-NOT-PLAYING — same REAL root cause found on creature-0001 and fixed identically here (proves it generalizes, not game-specific): an IMPORTED long AudioStreamWAV (~30s bed) silently won't play on the real WASAPI driver while SFX do; _make_player now REBUILDS the bed stream in code from the loaded PCM data + loops the whole sample + autoplay before add_child; bgm.wav.import set compress/mode=0 (PCM) so rebuilt data is raw 16-bit. Probe confirms MusicAmbient.playing=true, advancing. Supersedes round-1's same-frame-play() guess. (3) LEVELS: bed volume_db=-4 under the 0 dB SFX. Stable Audio Open (WAV, soundfile patch). Hard-edged chiptune identity (style B) — deliberate sonic contrast to creature-0001's warm acoustic. selftest SELFTEST OK; headless run clean; logic untouched. STATUS HELD (not auto-advanced to scored): awaits owner playtest + audio A/B. IP-safe: no artist/track names; music negative excludes vocals.
